title: Sanitize NaN, Infinite, -Infinite causing error when saving as PostgreSQL JSON
body: Bug fix: When query results contain NaN, Infinity, or -Infinity, Redash crashes while saving to PostgreSQL JSON. The patch sanitizes these floating values to None before JSON serialization, matching pre-#6685 behavior (simplejson ignore_nan). A unit test was added to cover this scenario. Sanitization is applied at the result persistence layer so all downstream reads (and exports) see JSON-safe data.

step_plan.summary: Sanitize NaN, Infinity, and -Infinity values before persisting query results to PostgreSQL JSON by introducing a recursive sanitizer and applying it at the canonical insertion point (store_result).

steps.operation: Refactor store_result to sanitize result payload before persistence
steps.reason: Introduce a recursive sanitizer that replaces NaN, Infinity, and -Infinity with None so the JSON stored in PostgreSQL JSON fields is always valid. This guarantees downstream reads and exports see JSON-safe data and aligns with the unit test coverage added for this scenario.
steps.side_effects: Adds a sanitizer utility (sanitize_json_values), imports math, and updates store_result to apply the sanitizer to the payload before persisting. May require tests to be updated or added; API/serializer outputs will reflect nulls in place of NaN/Inf; minor performance overhead for recursive traversal on large payloads.
